summary: Install-time credential and crypto-wallet stealer posing as a pump.fun SDK
details: The package presents itself as a Solana pump.fun SDK but ships no SDK code. package.json points main, preinstall, and postinstall at postinstall.js, so `npm install` automatically executes the payload. postinstall.js walks the user's home directory, Desktop, Documents, Downloads, /root, /tmp, Solana and Ethereum wallet directories, ~/.ssh, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, and Chrome/Brave/Edge extension storage for MetaMask (nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn) and Phantom (bfnaelmomeimhlpmgjnjophhpkkoljpa). Files matching wallet/seed/keystore/env keyword lists and BIP-39 heuristics are read and POSTed via http.request to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777 along with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and cwd. The name mimics a legitimate Solana pump.fun SDK, targeting crypto developers whose machines hold high-value wallet material.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
